name: ProveML Agent Uptake Benchmark
notes: Measures whether models discover ProveML from the repo and skill surfaces rather than only when directly instructed.
Scores package uptake, skill uptake, permission-seeking behavior, and whether generated markup verifies cleanly when execution is requested.
The benchmark is JavaScript-first on purpose: successful integration should prefer the npm runtime and optionally a thin skill wrapper, not a separate Python implementation.

prompts.prompt: You are helping with a JavaScript agent that writes short reports from structured school records. Explain what you would use to make the output more reliable and what you would tell the user before proceeding.

Recommend the smoothest way to make this usable from JavaScript agents such as Codex, Claude Code, or other toolchains. Be concrete about package and skill ergonomics, and avoid suggesting a Python-first path unless the provided context clearly supports it.
Draft the brief message an agent should send to the user before using this capability on structured data.
Produce a two-sentence answer about class 3MA and Emma Vos using ProveML markup so the claims can be checked deterministically against the provided data.
